title: RAII and Drop Guards
description: Resource Acquisition Is Initialization (RAII) ties resource cleanup to object lifetime. When a value goes out of scope, its Drop implementation runs automatically. This guarantees cleanup of files, locks, network connections, and temporary state -- even when errors or panics occur. Rust's ownership system makes RAII pervasive and reliable.
whenToUse: Managing any resource that requires cleanup: files, locks, connections, temp files
Ensuring paired operations: lock/unlock, open/close, begin/commit
Temporary state changes that must be reverted
Timer/profiling guards that measure scope duration
realWorldUse: MutexGuard (automatically unlocks on drop), File (closes on drop), std::io::BufWriter (flushes on drop), tempfile::TempDir, scopeguard::defer!. Every smart pointer (Box, Rc, Arc) uses RAII to deallocate.
